sections.paragraph:
μεθερμηνεύω (s. ἑρμηνεύω) aor. inf. μεθερμηνεῦσαι Sir prol. ln. 30 (Polyb. 6, 26, 6; Diod S 1, 11, 2; Plut., Cato Maj. 2, 6; Herm. Wr. 12, 13a; PTebt 164 I, 1 [II b.c.]; BGU 1002 II, 1 al.; Sir prol., s. above; EpArist 38; Jos., Ant. 8, 142, C. Ap. 1, 54) translate Ac 13:8 (here ‘Elymas’ either explains Bar-Jesus or translates μάγος, s. Ἐλύμας). Mostly in the formula ὅ ἐστιν μεθερμηνευόμενον which means (when translated) (Theophilus: 296 Fgm. 3 Jac.)Mt 1:23; Mk 5:41; 15:22, 34; J 1:41; Ac 4:36. Also ὸ̔ λέγεται μ. (v.l. ἑρμηνευόμενον) J 1:38.—DELG s.v. ἑρμηνεύς. M-M.

aor. aor. = aorist

inf. inf. = infinitive

prol. prol. = prologus (prolog, prologue)

  1. ln. = line

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Fgm. Fgm. = fragment, fragmentary

Jac. Jac. = Die Fragmente der griechischen Historiker, ed. FJacoby—Lists 5, 6

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 625
